sections.paragraph:
ἅπτω [cp. epic ἁφάω ‘to handle’; ‘make contact (with), fasten to’]

—1. ‘cause to be in a burning state by touching with fire’, of a lamp whose oil is touched by fire kindle, light, act. Lk 8:16; 11:33; 15:8; pass. Mk 4:21 v.l. In compressed narration, of a bonfire, act. Ac 28:2.

—2. ‘make contact with’, touch, take hold of, grasp Mt 8:3, 15; μή μου ἅπτου stop hanging on to me J 20:17. Of sexual contact 1 Cor 7:1. Of contact in a cultic setting, with μή expressing prohibition 2 Cor 6:17; Col 2:21. Of a hostile action 1 J 5:18.
